summary: src/read.rs's 7c2733c index hoist left 8 duplicate narration function pairs unconsolidated; explain() only reaches 3 _with_index twins, leaving 5 plain siblings production-dead

edit.description: 8 pairs, each a plain fn and a `_with_index` twin differing only in how the member match set is found (linear scan of by_kind vs. a pre-built member_index lookup), the rest of the body near-identical: why (345, pub) / why_with_index (463); impact (682) / impact_with_index (736); impact_one (1412) / impact_one_with_index (1545); context (1006) / context_with_index (1015); context_member (1111) / context_member_with_index (1146); context_member_one (1180) / context_member_one_with_index (1255); narrate_satisfied (1355) / narrate_satisfied_with_index (1379); count_satisfiers (1523) / count_satisfiers_with_index (1532). `explain` (240, the sole production dispatcher) calls only the _with_index trio for Species::Member (258/269/280) and only the plain impact/context for Species::Leaf (293/303) — each of those two production paths short-circuits its own member-grain-vs-leaf-grain branch (impact()'s target.contains('/') at 693, context()'s at 1007) to always take the SAME side in production (Leaf targets always contain '/', member ids never do). Net: `why`, `impact_one`, `context_member`, `context_member_one`, and `count_satisfiers` are reachable in production through no call chain from `explain` — they run only when a test calls them directly (confirmed: tests/read_verbs.rs, tests/prose_include.rs, tests/layout_prose_import.rs, tests/layout_edge_slot.rs call `read::why` directly; src/read.rs's own #[cfg(test)] mod calls `impact`/`context` with non-'/' targets at lines 2101-2305) rather than through `explain`'s actual dispatch — so no test currently proves the plain and indexed siblings stay in parity via the real production path. `why_one` (526) and `context_leaf` (1034) are the two helpers already correctly shared, not duplicated — the fix pattern to extend, per engineering.md's delete-or-subsume priority: collapse each pair to one implementation (e.g. the plain entry point builds its own index and delegates to the indexed body, or the reverse), never leave a second body a future edit can diverge from unnoticed.

tests.asserts: read::why (pub) narrates a member's rationale/edges identically after consolidation — its existing direct-call tests stay green

the inline #[cfg(test)] mod's member-grain and leaf-grain impact/context tests (2085-2305) — currently the only exercise of the now-consolidated member-grain path — keep passing with unchanged narration output

acceptance: No pair listed in files[].description keeps two divergent bodies doing the same lookup-then-narrate job; `cargo test` green including every cited test file, narration output unchanged (byte-identical) for both the explain()-reached paths and the direct-call test paths.
notes: Posture sweep, pipeline neighborhood (drift.rs/import.rs/read.rs vs. engineering.md+architecture.md; builtin_lock.rs/placement.rs untouched since ab01fb4, still covered). All other checked commits since ab01fb4 landed clean, no residue (full list in the plan commit body). drift.rs's tracked RawLockRow stale cite (open-questions.md ride-only list) re-confirmed still at lines 2144-2149, still unclaimed. scoped at b1defc9.

summary: main.rs's own RESOLVE_KIND_UNITS_COUNT/resolve_kind_units_count() is a dead duplicate left behind by fe58bc2's move to compose.rs — its count-pin test always measures a zero delta, vacuously green

edit.description: Lines 42-53 declare `thread_local! static RESOLVE_KIND_UNITS_COUNT` and `pub fn resolve_kind_units_count()` in the binary crate. The only site that ever increments a counter of this name is compose.rs's `resolve_kind_units` (compose.rs:597, `RESOLVE_KIND_UNITS_COUNT.with(|c| c.set(c.get() + 1))`) — a separate thread_local in the library crate, compiled into a different crate than main.rs's copy, so the two can never share state. Confirmed via `git show fe58bc2 -- src/main.rs` (MAIN-CORPUS-ASSEMBLY-TO-COMPOSE): that commit moved `resolve_kind_units` and its increment out to compose.rs but left main.rs's declaration and reader stub sitting untouched, outside the removed hunk. Since fe58bc2, main.rs's own counter has never advanced (grepped: `RESOLVE_KIND_UNITS_COUNT` appears in main.rs only at its declaration and its read, never a `.set(`) — `resolve_kind_units_count()` in main.rs always reads 0. Its test `resolve_kind_units_runs_once_per_kind_not_twice` (~729-783) computes `resolves = resolve_kind_units_count() - before` as `0 - 0 = 0` on every run and asserts `resolves < 30`: vacuously true whether or not `gate::gate` actually resolves a kind once or twice — `cargo test` confirmed green today with the pin permanently inert. Meanwhile compose.rs's real, correctly-incrementing `pub fn resolve_kind_units_count()` (compose.rs:44) has zero consumers anywhere in `src/` or `tests/` (grepped repo-wide) — an export nobody reads. Delete main.rs's dead thread_local + reader (42-53) and rewrite the test to call `compose::resolve_kind_units_count()` (compose is already `use`d in main.rs) before/after `gate::gate(...)`, restoring the real count-pin 630979f/fe58bc2 believed still held and giving compose's export its consumer.

tests.asserts: resolve_kind_units_runs_once_per_kind_not_twice reads compose::resolve_kind_units_count(), its delta over a gate::gate run is provably non-zero (n > 0 of the thing the test exists to judge), and a reintroduced double-resolve (undoing 630979f) would fail it

acceptance: main.rs carries no second RESOLVE_KIND_UNITS_COUNT/resolve_kind_units_count(); the count-pin test calls compose::resolve_kind_units_count(), asserts a non-zero delta, and cargo test stays green with the pin now measuring the real counter instead of a permanently-zero stand-in.
notes: Posture sweep, verbs neighborhood (main.rs/gate.rs/install.rs/bundle.rs/lib.rs/test_support.rs read whole against engineering.md+architecture.md; 26 touching commits since last full read, 6f862e7). Split traced via git show fe58bc2 -- src/main.rs. Every other touching commit verified landed clean (full list in the plan commit body). Rotation closes this tick (verbs is its last neighborhood). scoped at 1adf7f0.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (75) sits under a doc (71-74) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (650) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (677), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 548 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 647-648 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 672-675 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). f6731fb (DIRECTIVE_FIELD consolidated into graph.rs ahead of this region, in the d1af9a5..HEAD window) shifted every address below its insertion point; re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff), so every address above now carries at its new line.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1547) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1548-1552) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1550) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. Addresses re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff) after upstream graph.rs/test-file shifts; they carry at these lines.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0522eff and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. f6731fb shifted graph.rs's const region from 59/55-58 to 75/71-74 (DIRECTIVE_FIELD moved in ahead of it); value and cite date unchanged (5, 2026-07-02). `files[]` line cites rewritten to match. The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 0522eff.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0522eff, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 68fae5c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
